text.title: Solo Lead Qualification Rubric Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Solo Lead Qualification Rubric application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Score MUST evaluate exactly 4 axes: budget clarity, decision authority on call, sane timeline, no scarring contractor history. Adding axes is rejected.
rule.rationale: Solo BANT-equivalents drift to 10+ axes and become unusable; 4 axes preserve speed and discrimination.

rule.statement: Each axis MUST produce an integer 0-3 score; the rubric returns the sum and a verdict (qualify / triage / decline).
rule.rationale: Numeric outputs let routing automation act on the score; free-text disqualifies automation.

rule.statement: Leads without budget clarity (score 0 on budget axis) MUST be auto-declined regardless of other axes.
rule.rationale: Solo operators cannot afford long-tail discovery on no-budget leads; the lower bound is decisive.

rule.statement: Decision authority MUST be confirmed on the discovery call, not assumed from title. Title-only assumptions are rejected.
rule.rationale: Title rarely maps to authority in small companies; the call is the only reliable signal.

rule.statement: History axis MUST flag prior bad-contractor experiences (3+ contractors in 12 months OR named billing disputes). Flagged leads route to triage, not auto-qualify.
rule.rationale: Scarred leads consume disproportionate operator energy; the flag prevents misallocation.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
